question: How does the G900 Observatory tie admissible grammar steps back to quartz timing without giving quartz event authority?

numerator.name: sheets
numerator.definition: Steps of admissible action in the grammar.
numerator.authority: Only a receipted grammar rule, return-cell event contract, or later admitted structural rule may count a sheet.

denominator.name: seconds
denominator.definition: Clock time supplied by the timing substrate used by CPU, display, and stage refresh.
denominator.authority: Quartz scales timing only. It supplies no event authority.

unit_definition.rate_definition: Sheets per second is a readout of admissible grammatical action throughput over scaled quartz time.

boundary_laws.statement: Quartz supplies the timing denominator, not the event authority.
The grammar supplies the numerator only when a step is admissible by a receipted rule.
A display frame may refresh the witness surface, but a frame is not an event.
A timer may pace an animation, but a timer is not a structural cycle.
A counter may number updates, but a counter is not an event.
A renderer may display admitted information, but a renderer does not authorize marker lighting.
A marker gate opens only by explicit admitted return or gate-open authorization, not by elapsed time.

forbidden_misreadings: more ticks means an event occurred
more frames means a gate opens
more time means a marker lights
screen refresh is an admissible action
sheets per second is a force claim
sheets per second is a physics derivation
keeper_lines: Quartz keeps the seconds. The grammar earns the sheets.
The Observatory may read sheets per second, but gates open only by admissible return.
The quartz clock lets the witness surface move. It does not decide what has happened.
Lucky for me, CPUs run on quartz. But quartz only keeps the beat. It does not name the event.

interpretation.next_problem: Keep sheets/sec as a readout layer, or add a UI/status note that displays timing without granting gate authority.

criteria.description: The post-stub gate recheck exists, passed, and kept the gate closed.
The renderer fixture exists, but renderer enablement and marker lighting remain false.
The static G900 body is loaded as read-only source material.
Sheets and seconds have separate sources and authorities.
Quartz timing is explicitly barred from opening gates or lighting markers.
Sheets per second is a readout, not a force or physics claim.
